[2018-05-25 23:46] liadahlia (Lia): chess doesn't suck though
[2018-05-25 23:46] nvlx (Fate): but chess is a draw with best play (extremely likely)
[2018-05-25 23:47] liadahlia (Lia): but best play doesn't exist
[2018-05-25 23:47] nvlx (Fate): if a position is either drawn, won for white or won for black
[2018-05-25 23:47] nvlx (Fate): we could argue that best play is any move that doesn't change the evaluation negatively
[2018-05-25 23:47] nvlx (Fate): 1 c3 best play confirmed
[2018-05-25 23:48] liadahlia (Lia): isn't the best move any move that change the evaluation the most for you?
[2018-05-25 23:48] nvlx (Fate): well i mean, chess computers evaluate positions
[2018-05-25 23:48] nvlx (Fate): and chess players too
[2018-05-25 23:48] nvlx (Fate): but any evaluation that's not "drawn" "won for white" or "won for black" has some degree of subjectivity
[2018-05-25 23:49] nvlx (Fate): you could say that your winning chances are like 80% but what does that really mean
[2018-05-25 23:49] liadahlia (Lia): maybe it means that given all sets of moves anyone can make from that point on, 80% of them wins for you
[2018-05-25 23:50] nvlx (Fate): the problem there is that many positions are forcing in some way
[2018-05-25 23:50] nvlx (Fate): if there's a recapture, there's only 1 or so good move
[2018-05-25 23:50] nvlx (Fate): but it would look like the most narrow path
[2018-05-25 23:51] nvlx (Fate): but yeah, as far as chess playing entities are concerned, "best move" is the one that gives you the best winning chances
[2018-05-25 23:51] nvlx (Fate): whatever that means..

messages.author: liadahlia

messages.content: but chess is a draw with best play (extremely likely)

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: but best play doesn't exist

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: if a position is either drawn, won for white or won for black

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: we could argue that best play is any move that doesn't change the evaluation negatively

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: 1 c3 best play confirmed

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: isn't the best move any move that change the evaluation the most for you?

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: well i mean, chess computers evaluate positions

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: and chess players too

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: but any evaluation that's not "drawn" "won for white" or "won for black" has some degree of subjectivity

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: you could say that your winning chances are like 80% but what does that really mean

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: maybe it means that given all sets of moves anyone can make from that point on, 80% of them wins for you

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: the problem there is that many positions are forcing in some way

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: if there's a recapture, there's only 1 or so good move

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: but it would look like the most narrow path

messages.author: nvlx
messages.content: but yeah, as far as chess playing entities are concerned, "best move" is the one that gives you the best winning chances

[2018-05-28 16:00] thrarxx (Thrar): if that joke makes even 1% of players quit the game at that stage, it's a very expensive joke to make
[2018-05-28 16:01] thrarxx (Thrar): from a standpoint of developing the game's player base, i don't think it should be there given the risk
if not being P2W is a major "selling" point for the game (which it seems to be) then it should be absolutely, 100% clear about that at all times
keep in mind there are non-native speakers, people in a bad mood, people getting distracted, etc etc, it needs to be clear to all of them
[2018-05-28 16:03] thrarxx (Thrar): i think that sort  of thing is the difference between building a game for a niche group and building one for a larger audience

[2018-05-28 16:10] wilson0862 (Wilson): The question is do you care to keep these players who would quit literally right there and then. Like I said, the very next text box explicitly states that is not actually true.
[2018-05-28 16:10] wilson0862 (Wilson): Maybe you want these players to voluntarily remove themselves from your player pool.
[2018-05-28 16:11] wilson0862 (Wilson): I'm not saying that's the case; I'm merely stating you don't necessarily want literally every single potential consumer to be a consumer.
[2018-05-28 16:12] wilson0862 (Wilson): By the way, nice Chinese chess avatar. :D
[2018-05-28 16:13] darkeisbein: why do Asian chess variants have such horrible UI? 😦
[2018-05-28 16:14] awaclus: alternatively, you could just add a pop-up if people try to leave the mission during that joke
[2018-05-28 16:14] thrarxx (Thrar): from Lunarch's point of view, i'm not sure if there are any players they wouldn't want to keep as long as possible
i'm assuming the resource cost for an additional user are miniscule, and they don't negatively impact other people's experience
from my point of view the risk vs reward seems off
the reward is a small chuckle from players who get it, but potentially turning away players is very costly
[2018-05-28 16:15] thrarxx (Thrar): i don't think many players rage quit right then and there (although presumably Lunarch has that data), but it could be just another brick in the wall if misunderstood by someone
[2018-05-28 16:16] thrarxx (Thrar): @Wilson that is indeed a Chinese chess piece 😀
[2018-05-28 16:16] wilson0862 (Wilson): I haven't played in so long that I stopped recognizing them. :p
[2018-05-28 16:16] thrarxx (Thrar): @darkeisbein do you mean online or the game boards?
[2018-05-28 16:17] wilson0862 (Wilson): But anyway, I'm not going to guess what Lunarch's philosophy is on that. I'm sure they would appreciate that feedback either way.
[2018-05-28 16:17] darkeisbein: I mostly mean that for me not knowing the characters the pieces are hard to identify
[2018-05-28 16:18] wilson0862 (Wilson): Aren't there only like 7 different pieces?
[2018-05-28 16:18] wilson0862 (Wilson): I guess 14 if you include the other side.
[2018-05-28 16:18] thrarxx (Thrar): let's see... chariot, horse, elephant, guard, general, cannons, pawns, so that's 7
but they do have different characters on each side, so i suppose it's 14 that you need to be able to recognize
[2018-05-28 16:19] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yeah, five in the back, then two rows of units further up. Glad I haven't forgotten everything. :D
[2018-05-28 16:20] wilson0862 (Wilson): I do want to continue this conversation but can we use #board_games for this though?
[2018-05-28 16:20] thrarxx (Thrar): now that @darkeisbein mentions it, it does stand out that western chess usually has carved pieces while chinese chess has flat tokens with characters on them
